title: sempervivum tectorum f. alpinum
intro: Sempervivum tectorum f. alpinum, also known as the houseleek or living stonecrop, is a specific variety of the Sempervivum tectorum plant, characterized by its rosette-shaped leaves and succulent stems. Traditionally, Sempervivum tectorum f. alpinum has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its high content of sulfur and other compounds. The health benefits of this plant are said to include wound healing, skin protection, and the prevention of infections, particularly in the case of wounds and burns. In herbal preparations, Sempervivum tectorum f. alpinum is often used to create topical creams and ointments, as well as teas, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
